diff_summary: Adds GET/PATCH /admin/parents/:userId/auto-credit (apps/api/src/routes/admin/auto-credit.ts) to read/set wallets.auto_credit_enabled; grants admin `manage wallet` (rbac.ts + migration 0016); adds autoCreditToggleSchema (contracts) and a role-aware view helper (apps/admin/lib/auto-credit-toggle.ts). PATCH is gated by `manage wallet` (admin/super_admin), audited with before/after in-txn.
ac_coverage: AC1 (default FALSE) implemented (wallets.auto_credit_enabled NOT NULL DEFAULT FALSE, migration 0014) & tested. AC2 (admin/super_admin may flip; reception/cashier 403) implemented via `manage wallet` & tested for both flip and reject paths; UI affordance helper present and later mounted in parent-header (P1-E05-S02). AC3 (audit) implemented in-transaction with before/after+actor & tested. All ACs covered. Note: AC text said `parents.*` but column lives on `wallets.*` (single source of truth for the debit path) — intentional, documented.

findings.title: GET auto-credit is IDOR-readable by any parent (resolves wallet from :userId param, only requires `read wallet` which `parent` holds)
findings.detail: GET /admin/parents/:userId/auto-credit guards with requirePermission("read","wallet"). Per packages/auth/src/rbac.ts:73-76 the `parent` role holds `read wallet`. The handler resolves the target wallet from the URL path param (walletForUser(req.params.userId), auto-credit.ts:65-69), NOT from the authenticated session user. There is no surface-level staff guard on /admin/* (app.ts onRequest hook only logs; registerAdminRoutes is mounted with no prefix scope or preHandler). So an authenticated parent can call GET /admin/parents/<other-userId>/auto-credit and read any other parent's auto_credit_enabled boolean — a horizontal-authz / IDOR info leak. This directly contradicts the established platform convention: the parent's own wallet route (apps/api/src/routes/parents/wallet.ts:38-58) deliberately resolves the wallet from the session userId 'never a param, so a parent can only ever read their own wallet'. Impact is limited (a single boolean, UUIDs are hard to enumerate, and the write/PATCH is correctly gated by `manage wallet`), hence medium not blocker. The same shared pattern exists in the P1-E05-S02 reception profile route, so a fix touches an RBAC convention.
findings.fix: Decide the intended audience for the read. Options: (a) tighten the GET guard so only staff roles can read it — e.g. add a blanket staff/role guard to the /admin surface, or require a staff-specific permission instead of the broadly-held `read wallet`; or (b) if parents must read their own, scope the lookup to the session user (walletForUser(auth.user.id)) and reject mismatched :userId. Add a test asserting a `parent`-role session gets 403 (or only its own wallet) on this GET. Pre-existing-pattern caveat: align with the P1-E05-S02 reception route to avoid divergence.
